rxn_class_summary: Aggregation-induced electrochemical boronation–dihydroxylation of alkenes using water-soluble boronic acids as hydroxyl source in full aqueous media without transition-metal catalyst
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the lipophilic alkene aggregated at the hydrophobic electrode surface to generate a carbon-centered radical (then cation after second oxidation); the hydroxyboronate anion (R-B(OH)3-) supplies the hydroxyl via C-O bond formation at the aggregation layer

key_intermediates.name: alkene aggregated layer at lipophilic anode
anodic carbon radical from alkene one-electron oxidation
β-boronate-alkyl radical after first C-O bond formation

electron_flow: Alkene aggregates at the anode where surface tension drives concentration; anode performs single-electron oxidation of the alkene to a radical, which is captured by the hydroxyboronate anion to form the first C-O bond; a second anodic oxidation gives a cation that is trapped by a second hydroxyl from boronate, yielding the cyclic boronate/diol product which diffuses into the aqueous phase.
notes: Mechanism is presented as a proposed aggregation model (Scheme 2) emphasizing the well-defined diffusion regime: lipophilic alkene aggregates at the anode while hydrophilic boronic acid is shielded by water; the anionic product gains aqueous solubility and diffuses away, regenerating the aggregation site. Only the mechanism-introduction pages were provided, so no probe experiments (TEMPO, KIE, CV, DFT) are documented here even if performed later in the paper.
